parts.text: spawn("gitter", "Show me the style of the last 5 commits in this repo. Report subject style, language, and whether bodies are used. Do NOT make any new commit.") — that is your entire task. Do not do anything else. End the turn after spawn returns.
User wants me to spawn a gitter subagent to analyze commit style from git log, with clear constraints to only read and report without making changes.

input.prompt: Show me the style of the last 5 commits in this repo. Report subject style, language, and whether bodies are used. Do NOT make any new commit.

state.output: Spawned subagent "gitter#1" (session ses_1cf1d19a7ffe8Ao7KtXIll10ER). It runs in the background — you are woken automatically with its result when it finishes. abort("gitter#1") to stop it (only if the user asks). It will reply once, then be destroyed. Subagent slots: 1/8 — 7 free.

parts.text: The subagent was spawned successfully and is running in the background; I need to end my turn now as per the Orchestrator protocol.
Done. The subagent is running and will wake you with its analysis of the last 5 commits.

🔔 agent-intercom: your subagent "gitter#1" (gitter) has finished and been destroyed.
Its result:
## Commit Style Analysis (Last 5 Commits)

| Commit | Subject Style | Language | Body |
|--------|---------------|----------|------|
| `c6d47e3` | **No prefix**, just scope (`tui`) + version | English | **No** (empty body) |
| `f498057` | **No prefix**, just version | English | **No** (empty body) |
| `93958e3` | **No prefix**, descriptive subject | English | **Yes** (bullet list, explains why) |
| `398404f` | **No prefix**, descriptive subject | English | **No** (empty body) |
| `ad0e76b` | **No prefix**, descriptive subject | English | **Yes** (explains what changed + why) |

### Summary

- **Subject style**: Imperative/descriptive, **no Conventional Commits prefix** (no `feat:`, `fix:`, etc.), no ticket IDs
- **Language**: English
- **Bodies**: Used selectively — typically when explaining *why* or providing context (e.g., multi-feature commits), not for simple version bumps or single-line changes
Use this to report back to the user. If you need more work in this area, spawn a fresh subagent — the one above is gone.
